title: Domain-global namespace disconnection as binding constraint on global frontier resolution
hypothesis: Domain-first dispatch (formalized at 80%) + periodic synthesis sessions improves global frontier resolution >=50%

hypothesis_test.routing_hypothesis: FALSIFIED — already at 79% domain-first before any intervention
hypothesis_test.rems_sleep_scheduling: INSUFFICIENT — domain work does not surface to global because no linkage

governance_model.name: federated convergence
governance_model.principle: global frontiers decompose into domain sub-questions; domain resolutions trigger upward synthesis
governance_model.vs_rejected_model: scheduling model (adding synthesis sessions without decomposition links)
governance_model.implementation: when opening global frontier: define >=1 domain sub-question; when closing domain frontier: check if parent global resolvable

principle_candidate: Global frontier resolution is bounded by domain-global namespace linkage rate, not dispatch routing or synthesis scheduling.
